heading: LIMITATIONS, EXCEPTIONS, AND PENALTIES
label: III LIMITATIONS, EXCEPTIONS, AND PENALTIES
notes: Short TitleCertain provisions of this subchapter and subchapter II of chapter 15 of this title were originally enacted as section 3679 of the Revised Statutes, popularly known as the Anti-Deficiency Act. That section was repealed as part of the general revision of this title by , and its provisions restated in sections 1341, 1342, 1349 to 1351, and 1511 to 1519 of this title.Pub. L. 97–258

children.heading: Limitations on expending and obligating amounts
Buying and leasing passenger motor vehicles and aircraft
Commissions, councils, boards, and inter­agency and similar groups
Appropriations or authorizations required for agencies in existence for more than one year
Limitation on use of appropriated funds to influence certain Federal contracting and financial transactions
Acceptance of travel and related expenses from non-Federal sources
Limitation on use of appropriated funds for contracts with entities not meeting veterans’ employment reporting requirements
Prohibition on use of funds for portraits
